Crafted Pauldron  
 

Lore Item Quest Item
Slot: SHOULDERS
AC: 12
STR: +2
SV MAGIC: +2
WT: 4.5 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR
Race: ALL except IKS
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Crafted Pauldron
Lore Item Quest Item
Slot: SHOULDERS
AC: 12
STR: +2
SV MAGIC: +2
WT: 4.5 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR
Race: ALL except IKS
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No

Page Updated:Fri Apr 21st, 2017

Expansion: Original
Average Price: 130pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 25

Crafted Pauldron

Items
QuoteReply
# Jan 03 2020 at 5:16 PM Rating: Decent
Dabcloudz
3 posts
Evil eye prisoner also drops the item for the bracer so...2 flicks 1 frog thing.
▲ Page top
Effects
QuoteReply
# Oct 03 2002 at 1:20 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591781284498
15 posts
What the guy meant was until 50, the effects of stats is vanishingly small...
40 STR before 50 raises your mean dmg by 3% roughly...
40 AGI raises your AC by 8 roughly...
40 DEX means you proc 1% more often???
Warrriors and monks need to go with AC/HP armor for a LONG while...
▲ Page top
to the guy who don't need stats
QuoteReply
# Dec 27 2001 at 2:38 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591770398211
38 posts
The guy that told you that you do not need stats entill level 50 is a FOOL!!!!
EXAMPLE
DEX- Helps you proc the weapons that proc!
STR- adds a strength dammage and helps you carrie the heavy armor and loot
STA- Adds HP and we all know warriors with more HP are likely to last longer

These are just a few examples. Think about it before you make a finnal decision.
▲ Page top
RE: to the guy who don't need stats
QuoteReply
# Jan 29 2002 at 10:17 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766719130
59 posts
curious, is crested pauldrons better then these, i think they would be. +9 stamina....
▲ Page top
RE: to the guy who don't need stats
QuoteReply
# Feb 20 2002 at 4:47 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591765923498
Guru
170 posts
There are better shoulders out there that are pretty cheap or easy to get with better stats (Tattered Mantle for one), but if you there is a choice between this or Crested Spaulders, I'd go with Crested. Stamina makes up more than the miniscule bonuses you get from Crafted.

BTW, stats are not unimportant, they are just secondary.
▲ Page top
stats
QuoteReply
# Dec 23 2001 at 2:17 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966128487
6 posts
I personnally dont care about stats. A level 60 warrior told me that until like lv 50 you dont need stats but Only VERY high ac. so 12 ac isn't that bad
▲ Page top
A good alternative set of pauldrons
QuoteReply
# Nov 25 2001 at 5:41 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592245759462
Scholar
308 posts
For those of you who either can't afford to buy Crafted Pauldrons, can't find anyone selling them, or can't/won't quest for them, there is a good alternative set of pauldrons.

Engraved Bone Pauldrons
12ac 5agi 3dex -20cha all/all

Obtainable through a fairly simple quest in Kael Drakkel.
▲ Page top
To All With Crafted
QuoteReply
# Oct 28 2001 at 7:37 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758420318
2 posts
Well crafted is great i just finished my quest for it. to the lvl60 warr if you have crafted upgrade to warlord alot better or even champian if you can't do the warlord armour yet.
I plain on keeping most of it for a long time and i got it all at lvl27 not hard quest.

Good luck to all Warriors.
▲ Page top
Crafted Pauldruns
QuoteReply
# Jul 28 2001 at 9:01 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
These aren't that good. This quest is a time-waster more than anything. Shoulders are a good slot for MR for a warrior, so bloodstained mantle makes this look like the joke that it is. Crafted has some of the dumbest stats, but at least the ac is good.
▲ Page top
type of armor
QuoteReply
# Jul 20 2001 at 6:46 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591761526283
82 posts
crafted is considered plate armor so...iksars can't wear it even though they have warriors too.
▲ Page top
Crafted Pauldron
QuoteReply
# Jun 25 2001 at 11:31 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592757225820
13 posts
Crafted is some good armor that will last you until you get to the plains. I certainly plan on having mine that long unless my guild finds something nicer they dont want.

But as for the Pauldron.... I had an easier time gettting the Bp than this piece. But now that my druid is 31 and my guild is raiding on a weekly basis (if not two or three smaller raids a week) i should have no problem getting the runes... and R.E. drops some nice Alloy stuff now too.

Good luck all you warriors!

Nellen Flaminfeet
31 Druid
Sphere of Honour
Xegony

Phreezin
11 Warrior
Xegony

# Jun 16 2001 at 11:35 AM Rating: Default

Anonymous
This screenshot was pre-kunark, so Iksars can't use. It would be cool if they could though =)

How long with crafted?
QuoteReply
# May 31 2001 at 7:44 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592245845412
63 posts
I have a lvl 24 warrior with alot of crafted stuff, and I am wondering, what lvl should I stop wearing crafted? When does it get to bad for my lvl?

Taiddas Kavilion,
Halfling warrior of the 24th season
Xegony server
▲ Page top
Size of Pauldron
QuoteReply
# May 21 2001 at 10:32 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I have a question i accidently bought a small crafted pauldron for my warrior, but he is a half elf and cannot wear the small size, is there a way to increse the size from small to medium. I need to know cause i haven't found a person willing to trade a small for a med. If your on the torvonillous, pardon my spelling, and wish to help me give Komir a tell.
▲ Page top
RE: Size of Pauldron
QuoteReply
# Jun 01 2001 at 8:09 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592245845412
63 posts
I know I am suppose to give you a tell, but halflings can wear medium crafted, I have been told that changing the size just reduces the weight, so just try putting it on. And if it doesnt work (it probably will), all you have to do is give talk to the two dwarves in WC, they can "resize your crafted armor".
▲ Page top
RE: Size of Pauldron
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2001 at 1:08 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1652055378273
24 posts
That is incorrect. They can only make it from one-size-fits-all to small. Not the other way around. It is small and there is no way to change it. Only halflings, wood elves, gnomes, dark elves, and dwarves may use it now. On Torv, look for Gonwen in-game, he is interested.
▲ Page top
question
QuoteReply
# Feb 18 2001 at 12:57 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
this is a nice item with the 12 ac...although the +2 str and sv magic are pathetic. would any of you laugh at a level 60 warrior with one of these on? i dunno if i would =) imo this is a good item for any level because of the 12 AC anyone agree?

new question
QuoteReply
# May 04 2001 at 10:06 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
can i get some more info on the quest for pauldruns, i know you need somthing from runnyeye, can i get who it drops off and what exactally its called and about what level the mob is, thanks
▲ Page top
RE: question
QuoteReply
# May 04 2001 at 10:04 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I dont laugh at poeple with it on its cool looking and i see 50+'s all the time with it on, i am still looking for the Pauldruns and BP, i can buy the BP but pauldruns are hard to find. i have been wearing crafted armor sence about level 7, im a little bit of a twink but not really, all i have i used my friends druid to port for money and made the money my self. just yesterday i bout a Wurmslayer, at level 29 i love it and will soon be leveling really fast
Pularios warrior of the 29th season
Nameless server
▲ Page top
RE: question
QuoteReply
# Mar 29 2001 at 7:10 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591752367094
11 posts
I wouldn't laugh at any warrior -- we are really nice folks to have as friends!
Actually, I just sold mine yesterday for 400pp -- fellow said that was the only piece lacking for his full suit of crafted. Now, I have to try and recruit more guildmates to accompany me to Runnyeye once more to get them. They really are quite nice, particularly since they are questable and you don't have to pay item farmers for them with loot that is incredibly hard for untwinked warriors to accumulate.
▲ Page top
RE: question
QuoteReply
# Feb 26 2001 at 1:02 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591753934230
215 posts
I wouldn't laugh, personally, and not because I fear that warrior. I wouldn't because, as you said, it's a great item. Trust me, those bonuses may seem weak, but when they save your life because you could possibly deal that extra point of damage, or resist the spell that might have killed you, they're suddenly the greatest thing around. :)
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 04 2001 at 5:25 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Hey, I wouldn't laugh, but I WOULD feel sorry for that guy. A 60 using THIS is pretty sad. Rygorr has what? 18 ac? And some good stats, and thats EASIAR for a 60 to get then this hunk of junk.
RE: question
QuoteReply
# Aug 08 2001 at 7:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250248405
56 posts
Sorry - there are no Cobalt Shoulder Pieces. Kunark Armor is Head, Wrists, Arms, Chest, Legs, and Feet only...
▲ Page top
RE: question
QuoteReply
# Mar 10 2001 at 3:08 AM Rating: Excellent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
They are good. Im a 39 war..and i still have a bronze bp..now that is laughable. I have crafted and rygorr everything else. Just the BP is the tough one to find..hard to get anything really affordable. But at lvl 60 i would hope you would have some kind of cobalt..if there is a shoulder slot cobalt or at least some of the new velious class armor.

# Jan 12 2002 at 9:10 PM Rating: Decent

6 posts
I think you should try buying a Dusty Breastplate. It isnt very expensive, and same 22ac but with 5 STA and 20hp. I have on currently and I love it. Doh but wait, you might not be a small race :).

to the 39 warrior with the bronze bp. You should just go and quest a crafted, get yerself a group and do it up, not too hard)

RE: question
QuoteReply
# Mar 12 2001 at 2:33 PM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Cobalt is hard hard hard to get. I'm not sure if any "normal" player has more than one or two pieces if they're lucky (there's always exceptions of course). Anyhow, at level 45 I still wear these things. They do have good AC, though I hope when I upgrade I'll find something with some more stats...
▲ Page top
RE: question
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2001 at 11:55 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Well I'm in the mid 50's as a warrior and these are good but always looking to better them but not a priority. I have hunted a few times and many hours in Kael Drakkel hoping to get something there but as anything good it is always rare or very rare drops and finally when it does drop there seems to be 10 warriors with you wanting it as well. <praying for the roll of his life> but to end in you rolling a 5 in the lotto. Use what you get and be proud but always ask for buffs.
From a warrior with all the buff he can beg for but finally gets then can't see anything but the effect on screen.

Look and one day you will find

The Warrior
